sources.notes: Maintain the answer under exchange queries on two permutations.
Luogu solution treats the state as dominance components over two permutation coordinates and maintains component contribution under swaps.
Official editorial emphasizes the same greedy component invariant for exchange updates.

annotation.summary: Maintain a combinatorial answer while processing swaps/exchanges in two permutations.
annotation.constraints: n and q are large, requiring logarithmic-time updates of local component boundaries.
annotation.core_idea: View every item as a point with coordinates given by its positions/ranks in the two permutations. The reachable/exchange-equivalent regions form dominance components: after sorting by one coordinate, a component continues while the prefix maximum of the other coordinate overlaps the next point. The answer factors over components. A swap changes only O(log n) neighbouring boundaries in the ordered set, so maintain breakpoints and component contributions with balanced sets/Fenwick-like aggregates.
tricks: update only local breakpoints after swaps

solution_variants.name: dynamic-dominance-components
solution_variants.summary: Maintain ordered components induced by overlapping prefix maxima in the second permutation and update affected boundaries under exchanges.

tags.evidence: The hard part is maintaining ordered component boundaries and contributions under point swaps.
The invariant is expressed through two permutations and dominance intervals.
The maintained answer multiplies/sums contributions over independent components.
